Comment rules: Added a comment explaining why the line was omitted and that the default behavior matches the intended setting.
Loading rules: Instantiate LoadOptions with the default constructor and set the Password property (the constructor overload with a password string does not exist).
Remove usage of non‑existent LoadOptions and DocumentConverterPlugin classes; they are not part of Aspose.Words.
Load the document directly with the Document class.
Load the RTF source with `new Document("SourceDocument.rtf")`.
Document(string) constructor rule – loads a document from a file path.
Saving/Export rules: Ensure the output directory exists before saving the Markdown file.
Do not change the Aspose.Words API usage; Document.Save infers EPUB from the .epub extension.
Use Aspose.Words ImageSaveOptions with SaveFormat.Png and PageSet for per‑page rendering.
Iterate over Document.PageCount and call Document.Save for each page, setting PageSet to a single page index.
Use EpubSaveOptions (or HtmlSaveOptions with SaveFormat.Epub) for EPUB conversion; the dedicated class provides the correct API surface.
Keep the SaveFormat argument consistent with the option class (HtmlSaveOptions for Html/Mhtml, EpubSaveOptions for Epub).
Use a correct MIME type for MHTML attachments (e.g., "text/html").
HtmlSaveOptions(SaveFormat) constructor rule – creates save options for a specific format.
Document.Save(Stream, SaveOptions) rule – saves a document to a stream using the provided options.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Create the output directory before saving images.
Use ImageSaveOptions (or any other image‑specific SaveOptions) to define format, resolution, and the PageSet property for per‑page rendering.
Create image folders before calling Save to avoid runtime errors when the saver tries to write files.
Layout rules: Create a new section for each worksheet using `InsertBreak(BreakType.SectionBreakNewPage)`.
Write the desired worksheet name as the first paragraph of the section; Aspose.Words uses this paragraph as the sheet name when `SectionMode = MultipleWorksheets`.
Do **not** use a non‑existent `WorksheetNames` property; worksheet names are derived from the first paragraph of each section.
